# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

- About Us
- Courses
- People
- Events

The Department of Mathematics was started in 2016 together with the establishment of IIT Jammu. The vision of the department is to become a centre of excellence in Mathematics through high quality research and teaching.

**Teaching**

The department is offering various core and elective courses for all B. Tech. streams. Apart from this the department is also offering a minor programme in Mathematics. The minor programme in Mathematics is designed to allow undergraduate students to pursue a more rigorous education in mathematics.

The department has started its PhD program from August 2017. In addition, the department participates in the M. Tech. program in data science and information security.

**Research**

The department has at present energetic and dedicated faculty members in many diverse areas of mathematics. Currently, the main areas of research of the faculty members are Differential Equations, Numerical Analysis, Commutative Algebra, Group Theory, Number Theory, Finite Fields, Probability theory and Stochastic Processes.

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

The IIT Jammu Mathematics Colloquium is directed at students, postdocs, and faculty working in the fields of pure and applied Mathematics & Statistics. It aims to present expository lectures or mini-courses that appeal to our wide range of audience at IIT Jammu. One of the main objectives of these colloquia is to inform non-specialists and graduate students about recent research trends, ideas, and results in various areas of Mathematics, rather than focusing on technical details for specialists.

This semester (i.e. 2023-24, Sem-2), the IIT Jammu Mathematics Colloquium will take place every Wednesday afternoon from 3 to 4 pm. Invited external speakers will deliver exciting talks in online mode, while the faculty, graduate students, and the visitors of IIT Jammu will present talks in offline mode in alternate weeks. Please note that the timing for a few talks may be adjusted depending on the convenience of renowned speakers from institutes around the globe. Tea and snacks will be served after the colloquium talks.

If you wish to receive emails with news about the online talks and reminders, please contact the coordinators.

Coordinators:

1. Dr. Kousik Dhara

Department of Mathematics, IIT Jammu Department of Mathematics, IIT Jammu

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

MathematicsRahul Dattatraya Kitture

### **Rahul Dattatraya Kitture**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Hello! I am Rahul Dattatraya Kitture.

I completed Ph.D. from Savitribai Phule Pune University, Pune under the supervision of Prof. Ravindra Shripad Kulkarni and Prof. S. A. Katre in 2014.

I was a Post-Doctoral Fellow in HRI, Allahabad (2015-2017).

I was a Post-Doctoral Fellow with SERB-NPDF in IISER Mohali (2017-2019).

Currently I am Assistant Professor in Mathematics at IIT Jammu (from September 18, 2020).

#### Personal Website

https://sites.google.com/iitjammu.ac.in/rahulkitture

#### Research Interest

My current area of research is in **Group Theory** and **Representation Theory**.

#### Education Qualification

Ph.D. - S.P. Pune University, Pune (2014) and Bhaskaracharya Pratishthana, Pune.

#### Teaching Engagements

**Courses Taught**

1. **Galois Theory** (MAD00DP6E) for Ph.D (Jan.2024 - )
2. **Linear Algebra and Differential Equations** (MA-1-02(TO and SMD002U1M) for B.Tech (Aug-Dec, 2023)
3. **Research Study** (CCP010U2X) for B.Tech (Jan-May, 2023)
4. **Representation Theory** (MAD010P6E) for Ph.D (Aug-Dec, 2022)
5. **Galois Theory**(MAD00DP6E) for Ph.D (Jan-May, 2022)
6. **Abstract Algebra** (CSD002P5M) for M.Tech (Aug-Dec, 2021)
7. **Linear Algebra and Differential Equations** (MAL101) for B.Tech (Jan-May, 2021)
8. **Calculus**(MAL100 and SMA001U1M) for B.Tech (Aug-Dec, 2020)

#### Research Experience

- **Post-Doctoral Fellow** - **Harish-Chandra Research Institute**, Allahabad(Prayagraj), Sept. 2015 - Oct. 2017
- **Post-Doctoral Fellow** (NPDF) - **IISER Mohali**, Nov. 2017 -Nov. 2019
- **Post-Doctoral Fellow** - **Bhaskaracharya Pratishthana, Pune**, Dec. 2019 - Mar. 2020

#### Research Supervised

Currently I have one Ph.D scholar - **Miss. Sonakshee Arora (SRF)**- under supervision, who is working in **Character Theory of Finite Groups**.

#### Publications

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

MathematicsSartaj Ul Hasan

### **Sartaj Ul Hasan**

Associate Professor

#### Brief Info

Sartaj Ul Hasan was born in a remote village in the Shahjahanpur district of Uttar Pradesh, in northern India. He is currently an Associate Professor at IIT Jammu. Prior to joining IIT Jammu, he served as a Scientist at DRDO, Delhi, from February 2007 to January 2018.

From September 2011 to December 2012, he worked as a Postdoctoral Researcher at Carleton University, Ottawa, Canada, where he collaborated with Professor Daniel Panario and Professor Steven Wang. He received his Ph.D. in 2010 from the Indian Institute of Technology Bombay under the supervision of Professor Sudhir R. Ghorpade.

His research interests include various aspects of finite fields and their applications.

For more details, please visit his homepage:

https://sites.google.com/site/sartajulhasan/

#### Personal Website

https://sites.google.com/site/sartajulhasan/

#### Current Openings

**Open Ph.D. Position**

I am seeking motivated students for a Ph.D. position in the area of finite fields and their applications. Candidates with a strong background in algebra or number theory, and a strong interest in research, are encouraged to apply. Interested applicants are advised to contact me well in advance of the interviews scheduled by IIT Jammu.

#### Research Interest

Finite Fields and Their Applications

#### Education Qualification

Ph. D. (Mathematics), IIT Bombay

#### Academic Interests

https://sites.google.com/site/sartajulhasan/

#### Research Supervised

https://sites.google.com/site/sartajulhasan/

#### Research Projects

https://sites.google.com/site/sartajulhasan/

#### Publications

https://sites.google.com/site/sartajulhasan/

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

MathematicsSoumyadip Das

### **Soumyadip Das**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

I am an Assistant Professor in Mathematics department, IIT Jammu.

After finishing my integrated MSc from NISER Bhubaneswar I joined ISI Bangalore as a PhD student under Prof. Manish Kumar. My PhD work was on understanding covers of curves in positive characteristic, leading to my dissertation `On the Inertia Conjecture and its Generalizations' that generalizes and provides numerous evidence for a long standing conjecture posed by Abhyankar. I was a visiting fellow at TIFR Mumbai from September 2020 to July 2022. Subsequently, I joined CMI with NBHM in August 2022, and transferred to ISI Kolkata in August 2023.

#### Research Interest

Algebraic Geometry. Specifically, Covers of Curves, Ramification Theory, Etale Fundamental Groups, Orbifold bundles and Moduli Problems.

#### Education Qualification

Integrated MSc in Mathematics, 2009-2014, National Institute of Science Education and Research Bhubaneswar, passing year: 2015. PhD in Mathematics, January 2015 - September 2022, Indian Statistical Institute Bangalore Centre, 2020. Visiting Fellow September 2020 - July 2022, Tata Institute of Fundamental Research Mumbai. NBHM Post Doctoral Fellow, August 2022 - July 2023, Chennai Mathematical Institute. NBHM Post Doctoral Fellow, August 2023 - December 2023, Indian Statistical Institute, Kolkata.

#### Academic Interests

**Research Area:**Algebraic Geometry. Specifically: Covers of Curves, Ramification Theory, Etale Fundamental Groups, Algebraic Stacks, Moduli Problems.

**Teaching Interests**

Algebra, Algebraic Geometry, Number Theory, Topology.

#### Teaching Engagements

2021 - Math 147, Guest Lecturer at UP Diliman, Philippines.

January 2023 - July 2023 - Topology II, Co-teaching, CMI, Chennai.

January 2024 - MA-1-02(TO), Linear Algebra and Differential Equations, IIT Jammu.

#### Research Experience

PhD work, Covers of Curves, 2015 - 2023, ISI Bangalore. Post Doctoral Experience, Algebraic Geometry, 2020-2022, TIFR Mumbai. NBHM Post Doctoral Fellow, Research and Teaching, Algebraic Geometry, 2022-2023, CMI. NBHM Post Doctoral Fellow, Algebraic Geometry, 2023-2023, ISI Kolkata.

#### Publications

1. (With Manish Kumar) On the Inertia Conjecture for Alternating group Covers, J. Pure Appl. Algebra, Volume 224, Issue 9, 106363, 2020.
2. (With Huy Dang, Kostas Karagiannis, Andrew Obus, Vaidehee Thatte) Local Oort Groups and the Isolated Data Criterion Journal des Th\'{e}orie de Nombres Bordeaux, vol. 34, no. 1, 251-269, 2022.
3. (With Indranil Biswas, A. J. Parameswaran) Genuinely ramified maps and stable vector bundles, Int. J. of Mathematics, vol. 33, no. 05, 2250039, 2022.
4. Galois Covers of Singular Curves in Positive Characteristics, To appear in Isr. J. Math, arXiv preprint, 2022.
5. On the Inertia Conjecture and its generalizations, Isr. J. Math., vol. 253, pages 157–204, 2023.
6. Towards the Generalized Purely Wild Inertia Conjecture for product of Alternating and Symmetric Groups, J. Pure Appl. Algebra, vol. 227, 6, 107299, 2023.
7. On the classification of the Covers of Stacky Curves Preserving Slope Stability, Submitted, arXiv preprint, 2022.
8. Modification of jumps in the ramification filtration, file available under request.
9. (With Souradeep Majumder) Construction of the Moduli Space of Orbifold Bundles, under preparation.

#### Awards & honours

**Fellowships and Scholarships**

INSPIRE fellowship for higher education, DST (2009-2014), Institute Postdoctoral Fellowship, TIFR Mumbai (2020-2022), NBHM Postdoctoral Fellowship (2022-2023)

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

MathematicsTanmay Sarkar

### **Tanmay Sarkar**

Associate Professor

#### Brief Info

Heartiest Greetings to my Homepage!

I am a faculty memeber in the department of Mathematics at Indian Institute of Technology  Jammu.

For more details, kindly look into

https://sites.google.com/view/tanmaysarkar/

#### Research Interest

My research interest lies in

- Analysis of Partial Differential Equations
- Numerical Analysis

More specifically, I am interested in

- Hyperbolic Conservation Laws and Numerics
- Nonlinear dispersive equations such as KdV equation
- Discontinuous Galerkin approach to evolution equations
- Inverse problems related to PDEs
- Optimal control of evolution equations

#### Education Qualification

- **B.Sc.:** Ramakrishna Mission Residential College, Narendrapur (2007)
- **M. Sc.:** Indian Institute of Technology Madras (2009)
- **Ph. D.:** Indian Institute of Technology Madras (2014)

#### Academic Interests

**Research Area:**

Partial Differential Equations and Numerics. More precisely, I am interested in hyperbolic conservation laws and numerics, nonlinear dispersive equations, degenerate convection-diffusion equations, discontinuous Galerkin method for evolution equations, optimal control and inverse problems related to evolution equations.

**Teaching Interests:**

Ordinary and Partial Differential Equations, Numerical Analysis, Real and Functional Analysis.

Biography
Sartaj Ul Hasan was born in a remote village in the Shahjahanpur district of Uttar Pradesh,
India. He is currently an Associate Professor at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu.
Before joining IIT Jammu, he served as a Scientist at DRDO, Delhi, from February 2007 to
January 2018.
Between September 2011 and December 2012, he was a Postdoctoral Researcher at Carleton
University, Ottawa, Canada, where he collaborated with Professor Daniel Panario and
Professor Steven Wang. He obtained his Ph.D. in 2010 from the Indian Institute of Technology
Bombay under the supervision of Professor Sudhir R. Ghorpade.
His research interests encompass various aspects of finite fields and their applications.

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

### About Us

The Department of Mathematics was started in 2016 together with the establishment of IIT Jammu. The vision of the department is to become a centre of excellence in Mathematics through high quality research and teaching. TeachingThe department is offering various core and elective courses for all B. Tech. streams. Apart from this the department is also offering a minor programme in Mathematics.

### Message

We started our journey in the year 2016. Over the last few years, we are building lab facilities, growing our expertise and competence in the core Engineering  curriculum and research. Faculty is the greatest asset of the department and committed to provide not only the high quality technical education to our students but also the leadership qualities through which they can create employment to others.

##### Head of Department | Mathematics

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

**M. Tech.**

- **Core Courses:**

- Abstract Algebra
- Linear Algebra
- Probability and Statistics
- **Elective Courses:**
- Computational Number Theory
- Computational Numerical Methods
- The Mathematics of Imaging Sciences
